steps.title: Use two readable points on the fit line
steps.content: This is faster as a Desmos or graph-check problem than as a full scatterplot analysis. The line of best fit passes near $(12,480)$ and $(24,880)$, where $x$ represents temperature $t$ and $y$ represents sales $d$.

steps.title: Read the fitted slope and intercept
steps.content: In Desmos, enter the two points as lists and type $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. The regression row gives $m\approx 33.33$ and $b=80$. Since the graph points are approximate, that means the answer should have slope about $33$ and intercept close to $80$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choices A and B have slopes $0.03$ and $10$, which are much too small. Choices C and D both have slope $33$, so use the intercept. The line crosses the sales axis near $84$, not $300$. Equivalently, using $(12,480)$ with slope $33$ gives $480=33(12)+b$, so $b=84$. Thus the model is $d=33t+84$, choice D.
